_schema.purpose: Phase 1 spec §7 item 2. Editorial tokens-per-transaction priors per use-case archetype, low/mid/high. Multiplied by price-sheet rates and monthly volume to produce Layer-1 inference cost. ALL ranged and illustrative — the tool exposes these as defaults the user overrides (D4).
_schema.units: tokens per transaction (a 'transaction' = one user-meaningful interaction; defined per archetype)
_schema.note: Input:output skews input-heavy for RAG/agentic (3:1–5:1 per multi-model-token-estimates-2026-04-04.md). All tier 3C editorial unless sourced.
_schema.SUPERSEDED_BY: token_estimate_library_v1.json (2026-07-04) — this file is retained for the 18 Jun worked examples only and is no longer maintained. Do not dual-source.

code_assistant.note: Context-heavy: repo/file context inflates input. Interactions/dev/day is the bigger driver — set in the volume model, not here.

enterprise_rag_search.transaction_def: one knowledge query answered over retrieved docs
enterprise_rag_search.note: Most input-heavy archetype — retrieval stuffs context. RAG is where the 3:1–5:1 input skew bites hardest.

document_claims_processing.transaction_def: one document/claim processed end-to-end (may be multi-call agentic)
document_claims_processing.note: Agentic: a single 'transaction' can fan out to several model calls. Apply a call-fan-out multiplier (1.5–4x) in the volume model.

marketing_content.note: Stretch archetype #6. Cheapest inference of the set — sharpest illustration of how low the true floor is vs SKU pricing.
